Ad Text:
Senior Business Analyst / SME – Workforce Management (UKG Rostering)
Contract Role | Major Government Transformation | Tasmania

A major public sector transformation program is underway to modernise workforce and payroll capabilities across multiple government agencies. This is one of the largest workforce transformation initiatives currently in Australia, covering tens of thousands of employees across a complex multi-agency environment.

With the first phase of functionality now entering testing, the program is moving into the next major stage focused on Payroll, Rostering and Health & Safety systems. We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst / Subject Matter Expert with strong UKG rostering expertise to play a key role in this phase of delivery.

You will work within a large multi-vendor program environment alongside a global systems integration partner and senior program leadership to ensure workforce management processes are successfully defined, validated and implemented.

Key Responsibilities

Act as the SME for UKG Workforce Management / Rostering across the program.
Work closely with program stakeholders to capture, analyse and document business requirements.
Translate operational rostering processes into functional requirements and system design inputs.
Support solution validation, system testing and process alignment across payroll and workforce systems.
Facilitate workshops with operational stakeholders across multiple agencies.
Identify process impacts, risks and dependencies across workforce, payroll and HSE systems.
Collaborate with delivery partners, architects and functional leads to ensure UKG rostering capability aligns with operational needs.

Key Skills & Experience

Strong Business Analyst or SME experience in Workforce Management / Rostering systems.
Demonstrated experience working with UKG (Kronos) Workforce Management, particularly rostering and scheduling.
Experience supporting large workforce or payroll transformations.
Ability to bridge business operations and technical delivery teams.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills across complex, multi-organisation environments.
Experience working within large transformation programs or ERP environments highly regarded.

Work Environment

Location: Tasmania preferred, or ability to travel and stay 4 nights every second week.
Contract role on a major multi-year transformation program.
Opportunity to work on one of the largest workforce modernisation initiatives currently underway in Australia.

Apply

For more information or to apply directly, send your CV to: sam@spellerinternational.com.au

Principal Enterprise Architect - Data & Knowledge

TechnologyOne is on a mission to transform how enterprise software is architected and delivered. As the Principal Enterprise Architect - Data & Knowledge, you will design and lead the modern data and knowledge architecture underpinning TechnologyOne's Data & AI Platform. You will establish the platform's unified data models, semantic foundations, vector-based retrieval pipelines, and industry-ready ontologies - enabling trusted, context-aware, AI-led operations across all products.

What you'll be doing…

1. Lead the Data Foundation Architecture






Architect modern lakehouse and data-fabric patterns using Snowflake, Databricks, Palantir, and AWS native services.
Define unified metadata, lineage, quality, and interoperability frameworks.
Design reusable data products for internal teams, customers, and marketplace developers.
Drive scalable ingestion for structured, unstructured, streaming, and synthetic data.
Ensure strong data management and governance aligned to platform standards.

2. Build the Enterprise Knowledge & Context Layer






Design semantic models, ontologies, and canonical entities used across domains.
Develop and maintain knowledge graphs (e.g., Neo4j, AWS Neptune).
Lead architecture for vector stores, RAG pipelines, embeddings, and chunking strategies (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, ES vector).
Create the semantic and contextual grounding needed for AI assistants, agents, and analytics.

3. Enable AI, Agents & Intelligent Workflows






Integrate data and knowledge layers into AI orchestration, tool use, memory, and agent frameworks.
Collaborate with AI engineering on LLM/SLM switchboards, retrieval, grounding, and evaluation.
Support development of cross-module intelligent workflows for Finance, HR, Asset, Student, and platform-level agents.
Ensure reliability, observability, and explainability of AI features through strong context and data governance.

4. Governance, Quality & Trust by Design






Embed policy, lineage, and model/data governance across pipelines.
Implement standards for accuracy, explainability, auditability, and risk controls.
Partner with compliance and product teams to ensure platform-wide data and AI trust.

5. Leadership, Influence & Technical Excellence






Mentor engineers, data scientists, and architects.
Set technical direction, patterns, and reference architectures.
Work with product and industry teams to define workflow-aligned ontologies and data/knowledge products.
Champion modern data engineering, semantic modelling, and AI-driven design across the organisation.

Your Talents…






Deep practitioner experience with modern data platforms:
Snowflake, Databricks, Palantir, Lakehouse, data fabric patterns.
Hands-on technical expertise with metadata, governance, lineage, and data quality systems.
Strong architecture experience with knowledge graphs, ontologies, semantic models.
Practical experience designing vector search, embeddings, chunking, RAG pipelines.
Excellent understanding of cloud-native services, especially AWS (Redshift, Glue, Neptune, Sagemaker, Step Functions, Lambda, Bedrock/Anthropic models).
Experience working with or building agentic AI systems, retrieval grounding, or multi-agent orchestration.
Ability to translate complex architectures into clear platform roadmaps.
A builder mindset - comfortable designing, prototyping, and guiding engineering execution.

Why Join us?

TechnologyOne is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer who values diversity and an inclusive workplace. We aim to recruit a diverse range of people with a diverse range of talents to help us deliver on our mission to better our community. Our core values and culture unleash the boundless potential of creativity, innovation and the entrepreneurial spirit within our people. When you experience Life at TechnologyOne you'll see that our team enjoys an extensive array of benefits, including foundation charity activities, which all cultivate a vibrant workplace our people are very proud to be involved with.

Benefits…

Additional 2.5 days of leave per year dedicated to volunteering at a charity of your choice
Free breakfast and coffee on-site

Who we are…

At TechnologyOne (ASX: TNE), we're more than Australia's largest top 50 ASX enterprise SaaS company; we're a team driven by purpose! Job Title: Senior Account Manager – Energy Transition & Carbon Markets
Ad Text:
About RepuTex

RepuTex is the leading intelligence provider for Australia's energy transition and low-carbon commodities markets, delivering independent pricing, forecasts, and technology insights to support net-zero emissions decision making.

We work across three service streams: Carbon Markets, Energy Transition, and Power & Renewables. At the centre of our service offering is our EnergyIQ platform. Used daily by more than 1,500 professionals across over 200 organisations, EnergyIQ has become the “must-have” source of transition intelligence for Australia’s largest traders, industrial emitters, retailers and financial institutions.

In 2025, our clients accounted for over 85% of emissions covered by Australia’s Safeguard Mechanism and over 90% of traded volumes in the Australian carbon market.

Our market footprint has been built on the depth and credibility of our research. Our outlooks are widely referenced across the market – informing major policy, strategy and investment decisions, climate scenario analysis, along with day-to-day trading and hedging activity.

RepuTex was named “Data & Analytics Firm of the Year” at the Energy Risk Asia Awards in both 2024 and 2025 and is a recipient of the Australia China Business Award for energy and climate research across the Asia-Pacific region.

The opportunity

As Australia’s energy transition accelerates, we continue to expand the scope and depth of our intelligence services across our Carbon Markets, Energy Transition and Power & Renewables streams.

We are seeking a Senior Account Manager – Energy Transition to support the next phase of our growth.

This role focuses on owning and expanding a strategically important portfolio of corporate clients, while developing new relationships across organisations exposed to Australia’s evolving energy transition landscape.

Typical clients include large energy users, NGER-reporting corporates, institutional investors and organisations navigating new mandatory climate reporting under the Australian Sustainability Reporting Standards (ASRS).

The role suits candidates who enjoy engaging with the detail of energy & carbon markets, climate policy, and transition dynamics, with the ability to translate analytical insights into commercial value for clients.

Success in this role is driven by portfolio growth, cross-service expansion and targeted new client development across Australia’s energy transition economy.

Key responsibilities

Own and grow a strategically important portfolio of mid-tier corporate clients

Lead renewal and expansion conversations to migrate clients to expanded service tiers

Identify and convert cross-vertical opportunities across Carbon Markets, Energy Transition and Power & Renewables

Build relationships with large energy users and corporates exposed to Australia’s energy transition

Develop targeted new client relationships within defined industry segments

Work closely with the Managing Director and research leads on strategic client engagement and product positioning

What we’re looking for

We are seeking candidates with experience selling high-value services, intelligence subscriptions or data products, ideally within energy, commodities or climate-related sectors.

5+ years’ experience in B2B subscription or data-driven sales environments

Proven track record growing revenue within existing client portfolios

Experience managing and expanding mid-tier enterprise accounts

Strong commercial negotiation and relationship management skills

Experience in energy commodities, carbon markets, climate advisory or related sectors preferred

Knowledge of Australia’s energy transition policy landscape, particularly emissions reduction policy or climate reporting frameworks

Candidates from energy intelligence service providers, energy market advisory firms, research providers, and consulting organisations are particularly encouraged to apply.

Applicants must have full and unrestricted working rights in Australia.

Personal attributes

Intellectually curious, with a genuine interest in energy transition policy and markets

Ability to connect analytical insight to real client value

Comfortable engaging senior stakeholders including strategy teams, treasury, trading desks and executive leadership

Collaborative mindset and ability to work closely with analysts and research teams

Working with RepuTex

While RepuTex has been operating for over 20 years, we maintain the culture and pace of a fast-growing firm with ambitious growth aspirations.

Our team enjoys working in an intellectually engaging environment at the centre of Australia’s energy transition – with a strong focus on substance, curiosity and engaging with the detail of the markets we cover.

Members of our team enjoy a number of benefits:

Flexible leave entitlements

Flexible working hours and hybrid working arrangements

Relaxed and supportive working environment

Fitness centre membership (gym, pool, spa, squash)

End-of-trip facilities (bike storage, lockers, change rooms)

How to apply

Interested candidates should submit both a cover letter and a copy of your CV.

Note: Applicants must have full and unrestricted working rights in Australia.

Are you ready to be part of an organisation that values expertise, passion and diversity? At CFS we know that the foundation of our success lies in our exceptional people. We believe in celebrating individuality, have a passion for high performance and creating an environment where you can unleash your full potential. Our people enable us to make a difference and deliver exceptional experiences to help our customers achieve financial freedom.


Your Team

The Investment Governance team is part of CFS's Superannuation & Investments (S&I) business unit which is accountable for delivering investment, superannuation and retirement products to our customers. The S&I business unit is led by the CEO - CFS Super who has fiduciary and compliance responsibilities to ensure all CFS businesses manage risks effectively in our customers best interests.

Reporting to the Director of Investment Governance, this role supports end-to-end accountability for investment governance approvals, monitoring activities for products such as FirstChoice, Managed Accounts, and CFS Edge, and preparation of quarterly Board reporting.



Your Responsibilities

Conduct initial review of managed fund or listed security additions
Perform data analytics to support our ongoing monitoring activities of fund managers
Support the review of our investment menu of over 700 managed funds, sectors andcompetitor insights
Liaise with product and distribution to support the addition pipeline
Regular reporting activities as defined in our Investment Governance Framework encompassing both quantitative and qualitative assessments
Contribute to the coordination of quarterly board reporting to the CFSIL Board and Board Investment Committee.


Your Capability and Experience

Demonstrated experience in investment governance, portfolio research, or wealth/superannuation management, ideally within a trustee, platform, or institutional environment.
Bachelor's degree or equivalent in finance, postgraduate investment studies (such as CFA/CAIA) is highly regarded
Strong knowledge and understanding of investment products and structure (managed funds, SMAs and unlisted vehicles) across equities, fixed income, credit and alternatives.
Technical understanding and implementation of investment governance principles (e.g. SPS530), policies, and practices.
Strong understanding of the CFS business (or equivalent experience in a similar APRA regulated entity/business)
Previous experience in end-to-end delivery of projects /initiatives, and to manage cross functional projects.
Ability to develop strong working relationships across both CFS and within the team
Strong ability to solve complex problems, drive process improvements and automation.
Strong verbal and written communication skills
Attention to detail - Able to lead, pivot, roll up your sleeves and implement with quality and passion
Experience and credibility in engaging with senior internal stakeholders
RG146 or relevant other industry related qualifications

What to Expect


At CFS, you'll be working among the very best in the wealth management industry. It's an inspiring environment that encourages development and celebrates success. Other things to look forward to:

Access to CFS Employer Super, which offers an expansive investment menu and flexible insurance solutions
Support with financial services, including discounts on home loans and daily bank accounts through our partnership with a leading financial institution
Access to 24/7 confidential support covering safety, medical and mental health services
Additional leave day to celebrate your birthday
Life Leave - 3 days per annum to focus on your wellbeing or celebrate a special event
Exclusive discounts on retail, travel and entertainment

CFS Culture

At CFS we are committed to creating a thriving environment where individuals can flourish. We believe that success is built upon strong teams, and we are dedicated to celebrating uniqueness, championing individuality and supporting a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe that when you can truly be yourself, you can unlock your full potential.

Apply today and join us in helping Australians to achieve their financial freedom.

Please note, CFS requires all candidates to have full work rights in Australia.

Where we have preferred candidates, background checks (including Police, Employment, Bankruptcy checks, ASIC banned and disqualified persons) will be completed prior to the final preferred candidate's employment being confirmed. The outcomes of the background checks do not preclude the preferred candidate, however, they will be assessed against the inherent requirements of the role.

This role is based on Gadigal Land (Sydney).

Ad Text:
Senior Investment Associate – Growth Equity / Private Equity / Venture Capital

Sydney | CBD Location | $280k package + potentially carry

A highly regarded growth private equity firm is building out its investment team and looking to hire a Senior Investment Associate (equivalent to Associate Director level in many PE funds).

This is a rare opportunity to join a hands-on, partnership-led private equity platform focused on growth investing (not LBOs), working directly with founders, management teams and the Investment Committee.

The Opportunity

You’ll sit at the heart of the investment engine — owning models, shaping investment decisions, and tracking value creation across a concentrated portfolio. This role offers genuine pathway to IC exposure and long-term progression as new funds are launched.

What You’ll Be Doing

Build and own complex financial models (entry, exit, valuation, scenarios)
Run investment committee materials, KPI scorecards and portfolio reporting
Lead financial due diligence across new investments and exits
Maintain and evolve fund-level and asset-level models
Support exit modelling and capital raising preparation (VDRs, investor materials)
Work closely with founders, operating partners and senior leadership
Act as a trusted analytical partner across the full investment lifecycle
Background We’re Looking For

Circa 7 years' experience across either Private Equity, Growth Equity, Venture Capital, Investment Banking, Corporate Advisory or small cap investment analyst
Strong M&A experience - know how to run an M&A transaction from start to finish
Exceptional financial modelling and commercial judgement
Comfortable owning analysis end-to-end and presenting to senior stakeholders
Experience across valuation, due diligence and investment decision-making
PE fund experience helpful but not essential — strong deal exposure matters more
Entrepreneurial, proactive, intellectually curious and commercially sharp
Preference for multi-site, multi-jurisdiction service-based industry experience, however, a generalist who understands multiple industries also well regarded
Why This Role Stands Out

Growth equity focus — no leverage-heavy LBOs
Long-term capital, aligned investors, founder partnerships
Exposure to live deals, exits and new fund launches
Lean team, high impact, real responsibility
Enviable culture, the team really enjoy working together, collaborating and driving to outcomes
This role will appeal to someone who wants real influence, not hierarchy — and who enjoys thinking like an owner rather than a passenger. If this is the role for you please apply via the link or reach out to Dave Goldbach directly.

Job Title: Principal Strategic Procurement and Supply Analyst

Ad Text:

72.5 hour fortnight, includes fortnightly RDO
Hybrid, flexible working environment, including some work from home days.

Are you passionate about turning complex data into meaningful insights that drive smarter commercial decisions? Join one of Australia’s largest and fastest-growing councils and play a critical role in strengthening procurement capability across the organisation.

We’re looking for a Principal Strategic Procurement & Supply Analyst who can combine analytical expertise, commercial acumen, and stakeholder collaboration to improve how Council plans, sources, manages, and measures procurement activity.

In this brand-new senior role, you will transform procurement, spend, supplier, and contract data into actionable insights that support evidence-based decision making. Your work will help shape strategic sourcing, supplier performance management, compliance, and continuous improvement across Council’s procurement function.

Key responsibilities include:

Analyse procurement and organisational spend to identify trends, risks, and savings opportunities.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reporting that support both operational and strategic decisions.
Deliver advanced analysis that supports forecasting, category planning, financial modelling, and commercial negotiations.
Monitor supplier performance, contract compliance, and procurement KPIs, ensuring data accuracy and integrity.
Identify process and system improvement opportunities to enhance data quality, workflow efficiency, and reporting maturity.
Partner with stakeholders across procurement, finance, and operational teams to build capability and provide fit-for-purpose insights.

About you

You are a detail-driven commercial thinker who thrives on solving complex problems through data. You can translate technical analysis into clear, compelling insights that influence decisions at multiple levels of the organisation.

To succeed in this role, you will have:

Significant experience in procurement analytics, commercial advisory, or similar data-driven roles.
Advanced analytical capability and the ability to interpret large, complex, or incomplete datasets.
Strong proficiency with procurement systems, ERP platforms, and BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, SQL environments).
Experience evaluating supplier performance and monitoring contract and procurement KPIs.
Excellent communication skills to present insights clearly and influence diverse stakeholders.
A continuous improvement mindset and experience delivering practical, scalable process improvements.
Knowledge of public-sector procurement principles (Queensland-specific beneficial but not essential).

Why City of Moreton Bay?

What you do with your life matters. It’s the same with your career.

We’re the third-largest council in Australia and one of the fastest-growing areas. As Australia’s newest city, we’re building something that’s never been done before: a new kind of city, inspired and empowered by a new kind of council.

This is your opportunity to develop skills and experience in a truly unique place, at a truly unique point in time.

Get support when you and your family need it most with our 24/7 confidential counselling service, leader support hotline.
Our Strathpine office has local parking on site and is close to public transport networks, making for an easy commute.

Ready for a career that matters?

Join City of Moreton Bay, and help shape our city of the future.

ABOUT US 

Welcome to The Perth Mint, where golden career opportunities await. 

The Perth Mint is an iconic Western Australian organisation operating on a truly global scale. Situated in East Perth for over 125 years, The Perth Mint is a premier tourist destination, an award-winning exporter, and a key supplier of precious metals to markets throughout the world. 

There’s no organisation quite like it, with a variety of roles available, from engineers and chemists to artists and technicians. 

Foster your fascination and pride in our work. Get inspired every day by the world-class precious metal products we work together to refine, design, produce and then distribute all over the globe. Connect to the rich history of our industry in a workplace firmly rooted in the present — as leaders in sustainability and inclusion.

At The Perth Mint, you’ll be supported in a diverse team where differences are celebrated and your well-being matters — striking the right work-life balance with compassionate team benefits. And if your career goals change, you’ll be empowered to change roles with training and internal mobility.

We value inclusion and welcome applicants from all backgrounds, including First Nations people, people with disability, LGBTQI+ communities, culturally diverse groups, youth, and seniors. If you need accommodations, please contact recruitment@perthmint.com.

ABOUT YOU

Job Title: Product & Business Specialist (Lending & Credit Decisioning Products)
Ad Text:
Reports to: Product & Delivery Manager
Location: North Sydney (4 days per week in office)
  
Purpose of the Role
The Product & Business Specialist supports the design, development, and delivery of Elevant’s lending products and related platform initiatives. The role works closely with the Product & Delivery Manager to translate product strategy into structured initiatives and deliverable outcomes.
This role suits a senior product professional who combines product management thinking with strong business analysis capability and is comfortable moving between strategic discussions and hands-on delivery execution.
This is a hands-on role suited to a commercially minded product and business professional who thrives in a fast-paced fintech environment and can balance product thinking with disciplined execution.
  
Key Accountabilities
Product Strategy & Development

Support the development and enhancement of Elevant’s lending products.
Contribute to product design, pricing discussions, and commercial structuring.
Assist in translating product initiatives into clear business and delivery requirements.
Work closely with the Product & Delivery Manager to support product roadmap initiatives.
  
Vendor Platform Selection

Support and coordinate RFI/RFP processes for vendor selection.
Assist in vendor capability assessment, solution comparison, and selection activities.
Work with internal stakeholders to document business requirements and evaluation criteria.
  
Delivery Support & Documentation

Work closely with the Product & Delivery Manager and Business Analysts to drive initiatives through delivery.
Produce and review business and product documentation including requirements, evaluation artefacts, and supporting materials.
Support testing and UAT activities including building test artefacts, validation of configurations and platform functionality where required.
Ensure alignment between product intent, vendor delivery, and operational outcomes.
  
Product & Go-to-Market Support

Support product launch and go-to-market initiatives.
Assist in preparing product collateral, product documentation, and marketing materials.
Work with internal stakeholders to ensure product messaging and documentation accurately reflect product capabilities and value propositions.
Contribute to product communication materials used for partners, clients, and internal teams.
  
  
Stakeholder Engagement

Engage with internal stakeholders across product, operations, risk, and compliance.
Support vendor interactions and workshops related to product and platform initiatives.
Facilitate structured discussions and documentation to support decision-making for product initiatives.
  
Capabilities & Attributes

Business & Commercial Acumen: Able to connect product initiatives with commercial and operational outcomes.
Product Thinking: Understands how lending products are designed, structured, and delivered.
Delivery Orientation: Comfortable moving between strategy discussions and hands-on execution.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Works effectively with cross-functional teams and external vendors.
Commercial & Market Awareness: Able to support product positioning, collateral development, and go-to-market initiatives.
Ownership & Accountability: Proactive and committed to driving initiatives forward in a fast-moving environment.
Structured & Analytical: Able to bring clarity and organisation to complex initiatives.
  
Experience & Qualifications

Experience across product management, product operations, or senior business analysis roles within financial services or fintech.
Strong exposure to lending products (preferably consumer lending).
Good understanding of credit decisions, credit risk, and lending operations.
Experience working with lending platforms, loan origination systems, or loan management systems.
Experience supporting vendor evaluation, RFI/RFP processes, or platform selection initiatives.
Comfortable working hands-on with documentation, requirements, and delivery coordination.
Degree in Business, Finance, Technology, or a related discipline (preferred).
  
Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

Effective support of product and platform initiatives from concept through delivery.
Quality and clarity of product and business documentation.
Contribution to successful vendor evaluation and platform selection activities.
Stakeholder alignment and engagement across product initiatives.
Delivery support that enables structured and timely execution of product initiatives.
  
Ideal Candidate Profile
The ideal candidate is a commercially minded product professional who understands lending products and enjoys working hands-on across both strategy and delivery. They bring a blend of product thinking, business analysis capability, and commercial awareness, and are comfortable operating in a fast-moving environment where ownership and initiative are valued.
They are proactive, structured, and able to work closely with stakeholders and vendors to help translate product ideas into practical, well-executed outcomes.

Salary: $160,000 per year plus Superannuation plus Bonus
Ad Text:
The Company

Our client is a large, global, ASX‑listed industrial organisation with a proud legacy and an international footprint spanning multiple continents. It’s the kind of business that operates at scale, thinks long‑term, and backs its people to do work that matters - anchored by a strong focus on safety, performance, and continuous improvement.

This isn’t transformation theatre. This is transformation with consequences: clear targets, real initiatives, and senior leaders who want someone capable of bringing discipline, insight, and momentum to the program.

 

The Role

Reporting to the VP Global Transformation, you will play a pivotal role in tracking, validating, and reporting progress against a global transformation agenda—connecting initiative‑level activity to business cases, budgets, forecasts, and ultimately the results leaders are accountable for.

You will partner closely with Transformation project teams, Executive leadership, finance teams, and external consulting partners, operating across regions, cultures, and time zones.

This role is about far more than reporting. It is about bringing clarity to complexity, holding initiatives to account, and ensuring transformation activity translates into measurable commercial outcomes.

Key responsibilities include:

Monitoring transformation initiatives and delivering rigorous financial analysis on performance and value delivery

Reconciling transformation initiatives to budgets and forecasts, and actual results back to business unit performance

Partnering with finance and stakeholders on cost management and optimisation opportunities

Supporting and leading selected change initiatives across diverse cultural and regional environments

Developing and tracking metrics that demonstrate transformation impact and inform decision‑making

Preparing transformation reporting for the Executive team and Board, and supporting Investor Relations and external market reporting

This role is being recruited permanently and is Brisbane‑based, with global reach and influence.

 

About You

You are a finance professional who thrives in ambiguity and complexity - and knows how to bring structure, insight, and momentum where it matters most.

You are commercially minded, confident engaging senior stakeholders, and comfortable operating autonomously in high‑visibility environments. You understand that transformation is not an idea - it’s a chain of evidence - and you know how to build and defend that story.

 

Skills & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in business, finance, or a related field, with CA/CPA qualifications

Proven experience supporting transformation initiatives, business cases, or enterprise‑wide improvement programs

Strong financial analysis capability and an ability to align initiatives to broader business strategy

Excellent communication and influencing skills across senior, cross‑functional, and global stakeholder groups

Strong project discipline with the ability to manage competing priorities and deliver to milestones

 

What’s On Offer

This is a high‑trust, high‑visibility role offering direct exposure to Executive leadership, Board reporting, and Investor Relations.

You will be part of a genuine enterprise transformation where finance plays a central role in shaping outcomes - not just reporting on them.

 

Apply Now

If you’re ready to step into a role where finance sits at the centre of transformation, strategy, and delivery, this is a rare opportunity.

For a confidential discussion, contact Jack Gullo at the P&C Partners Brisbane office, or click Apply Now.

Ad Text:
Why Smart?

Look forward to working with Australia's leading and most trusted provider of Employee Management Services, making the benefits of salary packaging, novated leasing, and fleet management readily available to employers and their employees.

Working at Smart offers more than a job. It is a dynamic workplace that matches the enthusiasm for the work you do and ambition for an exciting fast-paced career. We have all been hired for a purpose and a reason.

Every day you will be part of a team that helps support fellow Australians - many working in essential services, corporates, government, and not-for-profits - to take home more money in their pay packets.

Do you have what it takes to become a Product Owner?

We're looking for a data-driven, customer-obsessed Product Owner to maximise customer and commercial value across our digital customer journeys. This role sits within our Salary Packaging & Employee Benefits - Product team and reports to our GM Product.

This is a hands-on role for someone who thrives on outcomes over outputs, owns the "why" and "what" of the backlog, and uses insight, experimentation and prioritisation to continuously optimise digital experiences across web and mobile.

As a Product Owner, you will own and optimise end-to-end digital customer journeys, ensuring all delivery work is driven by measurable customer and business outcomes. You will work closely with UX, Engineering, Business Analysts and stakeholders to frame problems, test hypotheses, prioritise value and deliver meaningful improvements — not just features.

Key responsibilities include:

Leadership of an agile delivery squad, working with your BA, UX, developers and QAs
Owning and prioritising the product backlog based on customer, commercial and strategic value
Translating insights into clear problem statements, hypotheses and prioritised backlog items
Defining and measuring success through conversion, funnel performance, engagement and task success
Running experiments (A/B testing, pilots and test‑and‑learn initiatives) with clear success criteria
Partnering with Product GM to translate product strategy into opportunities and executable delivery plans
Ensuring stories are "ready" for development with clear acceptance criteria, supported by the squad's BA
Balancing customer‑facing initiatives with architectural, risk, compliance and technical needs
Ensuring continuous value delivery and visibility of outcomes each sprint

What we're looking for:

A strong customer‑first mindset with the ability to simplify complex journeys
Confidence working independently while collaborating deeply across disciplines
The ability to connect business goals, customer needs, insights and digital delivery
A critical thinker and strong problem framer, able to make hypotheses, validate assumptions and make evidence‑led recommendations
Proven experience owning, analysing and optimising end‑to‑end digital customer journeys
Hands‑on experience with tools such as Google Analytics or Adobe Analytics, and interaction tools like Hotjar or Glassbox
Experience running structured experiments with clear hypotheses and measurement frameworks
Strong capability in backlog management and value‑based prioritisation (e.g. WSJF / DVF), including definition of ready/done
Understanding of complex enterprise technology landscapes, including legacy and strategic platforms
Experience in regulated or complex service environments (e.g. financial services or salary packaging) is a plus
Scrum Product Owner certification preferred
Training in Agile, Design Thinking or Lean UX is desirable

What's in it for you?

Smart team members enjoy a range of benefits designed to support flexibility, wellbeing, reward, and growth:

Smart Leave, an additional week of leave each year
Free lunch every week
Up to 20 weeks Paid Parental Leave with superannuation contributions and a Return-to-Work Bonus
Make a Difference Days - paid days off to volunteer for the community and environment
Discounted retail vouchers
Salary packaging and novated leasing benefits (of course!)
Free flu vaccinations
Employee Share Plan

Grow with Purpose

At Smart, we're driven by purpose and performance. We support individual and team growth via varied learning opportunities, open communication, and leaders who work to get the basics right - quality and regular one to ones, clear goals and success metrics, and growth conversations.




Show us you have what it takes

If this sounds like the opportunity for you and you have something to offer, we'd love to hear from you. APPLY ONLINE today.
Our Talent Acquisition team will be in touch to discuss next steps. If you require any adjustments to the recruitment process or are seeking part-time work, let us know—we're here to understand.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

We're committed to Equality, Diversity and Inclusivity. We welcome all people to join our team — including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, veterans, people with disability, people who identify as LGBTQ+, all gender identities, people of any socioeconomic status, age, race, national origin or ethnicity.

We're proud to hold diversity and inclusion accreditations, including:
- WGEA Employer of Choice for Gender Equality since 2019
- DCA Inclusive Employer 2023-2024
- Member of Reconciliation Australia's RAP network

Ad Text:
Senior Administrative Officer 1 - Remuneration Package Range $155,793 - $173,832 
(including salary $132,40 - $147,919)
Fisheries Business Services 
Darwin, Northern Territory
One full time vacancy, fixed for 2 years is available 

Join the Northern Territory Government and work with our dynamic NT Fisheries team, leading with purpose as we shape the future of fisheries in the Territory. 

Context statement 

Fisheries NT is in the process of delivering a major digital transformation program to modernise its data and information systems, replacing legacy platforms with a contemporary, integrated Fisheries Data Management System. The role operates in a complex regulatory and operational environment, requiring strong collaboration with internal teams, external development partners and fieldbased stakeholders. The position is central to ensuring the new system is welldesigned, usercentred, fully documented and capable of supporting mobile and operational needs across Fisheries programs.

The Role 

As a Senior Business Analyst, Data Systems Project Lead, you will provide strategic leadership, business analysis, project management and stakeholder engagement to support Fisheries NT’s digital transformation, including the migration from legacy systems, mobilisation of new digital tools for field use and continuous improvement of the Fisheries Data Management System.

Key Duties

Provide strategic leadership and expert business analysis to define, refine and govern Fisheries NT’s digital capability, including complex requirements elicitation, business process reengineering and enterprise level workflow design. 

Lead and project manage the end to end transition from the legacy Fisheries database to the modernised Fisheries Data Management System (K2), ensuring strong governance, data integrity, user readiness and alignment with program objectives. 

Provide expert advice and guidance to direct the creation, maintenance and governance of comprehensive system documentation, including functional and nonfunctional specifications, data models, process maps, integration frameworks, and user support materials. 

Manage the performance and delivery of the external software development partner, overseeing prioritisation, sprint planning, release scheduling, contract compliance and quality assurance to ensure ongoing system optimisation. 

Cultivate strong, collaborative relationships with internal and external stakeholders, facilitating strategic engagement, structured consultation, user acceptance testing, adoption planning and continuous improvement processes to ensure the system meets operational, regulatory and field based requirements.

Build the capacity of others and support the professional development of staff within the team for which the position is responsible.

About you 

Demonstrated senior level experience in business systems analysis within complex or regulated environments, including advanced skills in requirements analysis, process optimisation, and translating business needs into technical specifications. 

Proven capability leading or coordinating large scale digital transformation or system replacement projects, including data migration, user adoption strategies, and change management in multidisciplinary settings. 

High-level leadership skills with proven ability to work as part of a team, manage projects and change including managing ICT vendors or external development partners, prioritisation, time management, performance monitoring, contract management and quality assurance of deliverables. 

Highly developed analytical skills, with the ability to evaluate endtoend business processes, identify risks and dependencies, develop futurestate models, and provide strategic advice to senior leadership. 

Excellent communication, negotiation and stakeholder engagement skills, with experience facilitating workshops, leading consultation processes and conveying technical information clearly to diverse audiences. 

Strong understanding of government frameworks, including information management, privacy, procurement, governance, and compliance obligations relevant to the delivery of digital systems.

Asset Strategy & Insights Specialist

We bring the energy – through purpose and people

Not just through the infrastructure we operate, but through the way we show up for each other, our customers’ and our communities. The future of energy is in our hands. Let’s shape it together.




Are you excited to solve some of the most complex energy challenges of our time?

 

We have an opportunity for an Asset Strategy & Insights Specialist to join our team. In this role, you will transform asset condition, performance, maintenance and operational data into actionable intelligence that informs asset class strategies and investment decisions across AusNet’s commercial Transmission portfolio.

You will deliver advanced analytics, predictive investment and deterioration modelling, and executive‑grade visual insights to optimise lifecycle cost, risk and performance outcomes. The role strengthens asset intelligence through improving data quality, supporting failure investigations, and contributing to business case development and project initiation, ensuring insights are translated into practical, value‑driven asset management decisions.




What you’ll be doing in this role:




Analyse asset condition, performance, O&M delivery and defect data to identify trends, risks and emerging issues, supporting failure investigations and translating lessons learned into improved asset strategies and maintenance practices through clear, evidence‑based insights.

Develop and apply predictive investment, deterioration and performance models, delivering scenario analysis and long‑term forecasts that inform asset class strategies, asset replacement programs, OPEX work planning and prioritisation across 1‑year, 5‑year and full contract life horizons.

Strengthen asset intelligence by uplifting data quality across asset registers, inspection, maintenance and operational systems, implementing data governance practices, and working closely with data, engineering and operations teams to resolve data gaps and inconsistencies.

Deliver high‑quality reporting and executive‑grade visual insights, including Power BI dashboards and regulatory submissions, translating complex technical analysis into intuitive, decision‑ready outputs for senior stakeholders and external audiences.

Initiate and support capital replacement projects through the development of robust, evidence‑based business cases, ensuring investment recommendations align with asset management objectives and support effective project sponsorship and delivery.

Communicate technical trade‑offs, risks and insights effectively across engineering, operations, finance and leadership groups, influencing decision‑making and contributing to continuous improvement in asset management practices.







You don’t need to check every box, however we’re looking for a good combination of:




Degree in Engineering (Electrical preferred), Data Science, Analytics, Asset Management, or other relevant discipline

Post graduate qualification in Asset Management, Data Analytics, or Reliability Engineering (desirable)

Professional certifications such as IAM Certificate/Diploma, CMRP, CRE or project management credentials (desirable)

Strong analytical capability across asset performance, O&M, condition and defect data, with experience interpreting complex infrastructure datasets to identify risks, trends and actionable insights, including predictive, investment and lifecycle modelling with cost–risk–performance trade‑off evaluation.

Advanced data visualisation, reporting and asset information management experience, including Power BI and the integration of data across asset, inspection, maintenance and operational systems (e.g. SAP/Maximo, GIS), with the ability to translate complex technical analysis into clear, decision‑ready insights for senior stakeholders.

Sound asset management knowledge, including lifecycle planning, asset strategies, deterioration behaviour and maintenance strategy development, with familiarity in regulatory, assurance and compliance frameworks relevant to energy networks or large public infrastructure.

Strong stakeholder engagement, communication and influencing skills, able to work effectively across engineering, operations, maintenance, finance and leadership groups, supporting business case development and influencing outcomes through evidence‑based insight rather than authority.

Demonstrated ability to operate as a senior technical specialist, leading or contributing to asset management improvement initiatives, independently managing priorities while aligning work to broader asset strategy objectives.

Highly developed interpersonal capability, including relationship building, facilitation, constructive challenge and systems thinking, with a proactive, curious mindset and the ability to navigate ambiguity to support balanced, value‑driven decisions.







The AusNet experience is all about…

 

Growing your impact: We’ll support you to build your career through real opportunities and strong connections.

Solving some of the most complex energy challenges of our time: We love unpacking complex challenges in clever ways and looking at things differently.

Being apart a team that cares deeply about each other: We share in the good times. We support in the tough times. And we’re humble through and through.

 

 

We’re committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce. We believe different perspectives are essential to our success, and we encourage all applicants to apply. If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, we're happy to discuss how we can support you.




AusNet. We bring the energy. Want to be part of it too? Apply now.

Job Title: Principal Data Engineer - NSW Procurement Data & Analytics
Ad Text:
Principal Data Engineer

NSW Procurement Data & Analytics

 

·       Employment Type: Ongoing

·       Classification / Grade / Band:  Clerk Grade 11/12

·       Remuneration: $149,739 - $173,174; plus, Superannuation and Leave Loading

·       Location: Sydney CBD, Flexible & hybrid working options available

·       Applications Close: Monday, 30 March 2026 at 9:59am

 

Stepping into the heart of the NSW Government 

You will have an invaluable opportunity to create a positive impact for the people of New South Wales. You will be challenged to provide the best outcomes possible in this role while developing and growing your own skills.

When you join Treasury, you join an organisation that is a whole-of-government financial and economic advisor. Treasury is a critical component of the government and provides advice that informs a wide range of financial, economic and policy driven work for the state of NSW.

We consider work-life balance a key feature of being employed here. You will have the flexibility to manage your work schedule around times that best suit your life outside of the workplace and in line with organisational needs.

 

Enrich your career with the NSW Treasury

While we are certain you will already have your own expertise to ensure success in this role, you will also continue to learn and improve your skills in core areas, including:

·       Technical Skills including database best practice, ETL tools and cloud products

·       Data Analysis and Interpretation

·       Stakeholder management

You will join our NSW Procurement Data and Analytics team and will be working within the Procurement Reform and Banking Group. Read more about your future Group and Division.

As you grow in your role at Treasury, there may be opportunities to be a subject matter expert and to be a key contact for the business group. This may lead to opportunities to move into leadership.

Transform and develop your career in a central government agency that is invested in you.

The Principal Data Engineer role will provide you with the opportunity to expand your network as you will have the chance to work with multiple teams, agencies, external stakeholders, and organisations to help shape your career.

 

Investing in you

The team you will join is made up of a diverse and welcoming group of people. We understand what starting a new job is like, and to ensure you have the most positive experience we will ‘Buddy’ you up with a team member to help settle you in.

Just some of the benefits of working with Treasury include:

·       Flexible hybrid working at our office at 52 Martin Place, Sydney with standard working hours 35 hours per week.

·       Accrue quarterly flex time for any hours worked over the standard hours, and enjoy true work life balance

·       Continuous learning initiatives to invest in your career. We invite subject matter experts to share their knowledge and expertise during in-house education sessions, and online training is at your fingertips to access.

·       A healthy workplace, both mentally and physically. Our Wellbeing at Treasury curriculum offers regular seminars. From opportunities to connect with colleagues to boost social connection, to seminars on creating better financial health and physical and psychological wellbeing. 

·       A range of leave to support you with your needs out of work including generous paid parental leave, carers leave and more.

 

Read more about our benefits.

 

Your day as a Principal Data Engineer

In this role, you will design, build and continuously improve secure, scalable technology and delivery pipelines that enable consistent, whole‑of‑government procurement and financial insights. Your work will strengthen visibility and assurance over NSW Government Election Commitments, supporting both social and economic priorities, and enabling evidence‑based decision‑making. By partnering across Procurement, Policy, Banking and Finance, and Strategy functions, you will help uplift governance, improve outcomes for citizens and suppliers, and support the Procurement Board’s strategic objectives of transparency, efficiency, innovation and value for money.

This role liaises extensively with other areas within Treasury, with NSW government agencies and equivalents in other jurisdictions across Australia, and with external stakeholders.

 

Achieve success as a Principal Data Engineer

In this integral role there are a few things we would like for you to have to best support yourself and add value to the people of NSW.

You will be successful in this role if you are: 

·       You are technically minded. You have a strong background in IT and have experience with technology stacks, solution architecting, system migration, programming, analytics and analytics tools, and a good working knowledge of Machine Learning concepts

·       You are skilled in the delivery of AI solutions. You have successfully translated emerging AI capabilities into production-ready solutions, ensuring models are governed, explainable, secure and architected for scale across the enterprise data ecosystem.

·       You have excellent communication skills. Be able to concisely communicate with both technical and non-technical audiences and be able to adjust communication styles to the audience.

·       You are a strategic thinker. Identifies areas for efficiency improvements and automation, reimagines and develops solutions, avoiding the rhythm of entrenched practices and processes

·       You are a collaborator. Partners with Treasury colleagues, subject matter experts and stakeholders on requirements design and delivery, optimisation and continual improvement to ensure the successful management of analytics platforms

·       You have excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.  You can assimilate information quickly and derive practical, timely solutions that focus on outcomes

·       You are evolving into an effective leader.  You have some experience supervising staff, determining operational priorities, monitoring, and reviewing performance and providing leadership and guidance to ensure staff have the knowledge, skills, and support to achieve objectives and maintain a focus on delivering quality outcomes

·       You strive for high standards. Understand the importance of enterprise grade analytics products that can handle the full breath of government expenditure transactions to meet the highest standards of probity and public scrutiny and relevant regulations

 

Interested in working with us?

We would love to learn more about you!

We welcome applications from all ages and genders, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, LGBTQI+ and diverse ethnic and cultural backgrounds.

Read the role description to learn more about the role and submit your application ONLY via the I Work for NSW jobs platform.

NSW Treasury is an accredited Disability Confident Recruiter. This means we provide a fair and equitable recruitment process to all candidates. People with a disability can apply for our jobs without experiencing barriers. We are committed to being an employer of choice for the people of New South Wales.

NSW Treasury is accredited as a Level 1 Activate Carer Employer.

We are committed to being an employer of choice for the people of NSW.

 

How we get to know you:

·       An up-to-date resume detailing your knowledge, skills, and experience in relation to the role.

·       Complete several pre-screening questions as part of the application process.

·       A cover letter which must also include your response to the following questions:

Asset Strategy & Insights Specialist

We bring the energy – through purpose and people

Not just through the infrastructure we operate, but through the way we show up for each other, our customers’ and our communities. The future of energy is in our hands. Let’s shape it together.




Are you excited to solve some of the most complex energy challenges of our time?

 

We have an opportunity for an Asset Strategy & Insights Specialist to join our team. In this role, you will transform asset condition, performance, maintenance and operational data into actionable intelligence that informs asset class strategies and investment decisions across AusNet’s commercial Transmission portfolio.

You will deliver advanced analytics, predictive investment and deterioration modelling, and executive‑grade visual insights to optimise lifecycle cost, risk and performance outcomes. The role strengthens asset intelligence through improving data quality, supporting failure investigations, and contributing to business case development and project initiation, ensuring insights are translated into practical, value‑driven asset management decisions.




What you’ll be doing in this role:




Analyse asset condition, performance, O&M delivery and defect data to identify trends, risks and emerging issues, supporting failure investigations and translating lessons learned into improved asset strategies and maintenance practices through clear, evidence‑based insights.

Develop and apply predictive investment, deterioration and performance models, delivering scenario analysis and long‑term forecasts that inform asset class strategies, asset replacement programs, OPEX work planning and prioritisation across 1‑year, 5‑year and full contract life horizons.

Strengthen asset intelligence by uplifting data quality across asset registers, inspection, maintenance and operational systems, implementing data governance practices, and working closely with data, engineering and operations teams to resolve data gaps and inconsistencies.

Deliver high‑quality reporting and executive‑grade visual insights, including Power BI dashboards and regulatory submissions, translating complex technical analysis into intuitive, decision‑ready outputs for senior stakeholders and external audiences.

Initiate and support capital replacement projects through the development of robust, evidence‑based business cases, ensuring investment recommendations align with asset management objectives and support effective project sponsorship and delivery.

Communicate technical trade‑offs, risks and insights effectively across engineering, operations, finance and leadership groups, influencing decision‑making and contributing to continuous improvement in asset management practices.







You don’t need to check every box, however we’re looking for a good combination of:




Degree in Engineering (Electrical preferred), Data Science, Analytics, Asset Management, or other relevant discipline

Post graduate qualification in Asset Management, Data Analytics, or Reliability Engineering (desirable)

Professional certifications such as IAM Certificate/Diploma, CMRP, CRE or project management credentials (desirable)

Strong analytical capability across asset performance, O&M, condition and defect data, with experience interpreting complex infrastructure datasets to identify risks, trends and actionable insights, including predictive, investment and lifecycle modelling with cost–risk–performance trade‑off evaluation.

Advanced data visualisation, reporting and asset information management experience, including Power BI and the integration of data across asset, inspection, maintenance and operational systems (e.g. SAP/Maximo, GIS), with the ability to translate complex technical analysis into clear, decision‑ready insights for senior stakeholders.

Sound asset management knowledge, including lifecycle planning, asset strategies, deterioration behaviour and maintenance strategy development, with familiarity in regulatory, assurance and compliance frameworks relevant to energy networks or large public infrastructure.

Strong stakeholder engagement, communication and influencing skills, able to work effectively across engineering, operations, maintenance, finance and leadership groups, supporting business case development and influencing outcomes through evidence‑based insight rather than authority.

Demonstrated ability to operate as a senior technical specialist, leading or contributing to asset management improvement initiatives, independently managing priorities while aligning work to broader asset strategy objectives.

Highly developed interpersonal capability, including relationship building, facilitation, constructive challenge and systems thinking, with a proactive, curious mindset and the ability to navigate ambiguity to support balanced, value‑driven decisions.







The AusNet experience is all about…

 

Growing your impact: We’ll support you to build your career through real opportunities and strong connections.

Solving some of the most complex energy challenges of our time: We love unpacking complex challenges in clever ways and looking at things differently.

Being apart a team that cares deeply about each other: We share in the good times. We support in the tough times. And we’re humble through and through.

 

 

We’re committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce. We believe different perspectives are essential to our success, and we encourage all applicants to apply. If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, we're happy to discuss how we can support you.




AusNet. We bring the energy. Want to be part of it too? Apply now.

Job Title: Principal Data Engineer - NSW Procurement Data & Analytics
Ad Text:
Principal Data Engineer

NSW Procurement Data & Analytics

Stepping into the heart of the NSW Government 

You will have an invaluable opportunity to create a positive impact for the people of New South Wales. You will be challenged to provide the best outcomes possible in this role while developing and growing your own skills.

When you join Treasury, you join an organisation that is a whole-of-government financial and economic advisor. Treasury is a critical component of the government and provides advice that informs a wide range of financial, economic and policy driven work for the state of NSW.

We consider work-life balance a key feature of being employed here. You will have the flexibility to manage your work schedule around times that best suit your life outside of the workplace and in line with organisational needs.

 

Enrich your career with the NSW Treasury

While we are certain you will already have your own expertise to ensure success in this role, you will also continue to learn and improve your skills in core areas, including:

·       Technical Skills including database best practice, ETL tools and cloud products

·       Data Analysis and Interpretation

·       Stakeholder management

You will join our NSW Procurement Data and Analytics team and will be working within the Procurement Reform and Banking Group. Read more about your future Group and Division.

As you grow in your role at Treasury, there may be opportunities to be a subject matter expert and to be a key contact for the business group. This may lead to opportunities to move into leadership.

Transform and develop your career in a central government agency that is invested in you.

The Principal Data Engineer role will provide you with the opportunity to expand your network as you will have the chance to work with multiple teams, agencies, external stakeholders, and organisations to help shape your career.

 

Investing in you

The team you will join is made up of a diverse and welcoming group of people. We understand what starting a new job is like, and to ensure you have the most positive experience we will ‘Buddy’ you up with a team member to help settle you in.

Just some of the benefits of working with Treasury include:

·       Flexible hybrid working at our office at 52 Martin Place, Sydney with standard working hours 35 hours per week.

·       Accrue quarterly flex time for any hours worked over the standard hours, and enjoy true work life balance

·       Continuous learning initiatives to invest in your career. We invite subject matter experts to share their knowledge and expertise during in-house education sessions, and online training is at your fingertips to access.

·       A healthy workplace, both mentally and physically. Our Wellbeing at Treasury curriculum offers regular seminars. From opportunities to connect with colleagues to boost social connection, to seminars on creating better financial health and physical and psychological wellbeing. 

·       A range of leave to support you with your needs out of work including generous paid parental leave, carers leave and more.

 

Read more about our benefits.

 

Your day as a Principal Data Engineer

In this role, you will design, build and continuously improve secure, scalable technology and delivery pipelines that enable consistent, whole‑of‑government procurement and financial insights. Your work will strengthen visibility and assurance over NSW Government Election Commitments, supporting both social and economic priorities, and enabling evidence‑based decision‑making. By partnering across Procurement, Policy, Banking and Finance, and Strategy functions, you will help uplift governance, improve outcomes for citizens and suppliers, and support the Procurement Board’s strategic objectives of transparency, efficiency, innovation and value for money.

This role liaises extensively with other areas within Treasury, with NSW government agencies and equivalents in other jurisdictions across Australia, and with external stakeholders.

 

Achieve success as a Principal Data Engineer

In this integral role there are a few things we would like for you to have to best support yourself and add value to the people of NSW.

You will be successful in this role if you are: 

·       You are technically minded. You have a strong background in IT and have experience with technology stacks, solution architecting, system migration, programming, analytics and analytics tools, and a good working knowledge of Machine Learning concepts

·       You are skilled in the delivery of AI solutions. You have successfully translated emerging AI capabilities into production-ready solutions, ensuring models are governed, explainable, secure and architected for scale across the enterprise data ecosystem.

·       You have excellent communication skills. Be able to concisely communicate with both technical and non-technical audiences and be able to adjust communication styles to the audience.

·       You are a strategic thinker. Identifies areas for efficiency improvements and automation, reimagines and develops solutions, avoiding the rhythm of entrenched practices and processes

·       You are a collaborator. Partners with Treasury colleagues, subject matter experts and stakeholders on requirements design and delivery, optimisation and continual improvement to ensure the successful management of analytics platforms

·       You have excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.  You can assimilate information quickly and derive practical, timely solutions that focus on outcomes

·       You are evolving into an effective leader.  You have some experience supervising staff, determining operational priorities, monitoring, and reviewing performance and providing leadership and guidance to ensure staff have the knowledge, skills, and support to achieve objectives and maintain a focus on delivering quality outcomes

·       You strive for high standards. Understand the importance of enterprise grade analytics products that can handle the full breath of government expenditure transactions to meet the highest standards of probity and public scrutiny and relevant regulations

Join the change. Electrify your future!
We've been proudly Aussie since 1837, always finding new ways to innovate in energy and essential services. Now, we're moving towards a sustainable future through electrification and investing in renewable energy-and we'd like you to join us. Whether you're on-site, in the office, or somewhere in between, you'll find opportunities to grow your career here. You'll work with a team that's passionate about powering Australian lives, in a culture that values inclusivity, respect, and learning. Help us create a cleaner energy future, and we'll back you every step of the way as you build your career.

Join the change. Electrify your future.

About the role
Our Electrification & Innovation (E&I) team is driving Australia's clean energy future by developing sustainable, scalable products that support the nation's transition to net zero. As the Program Delivery Manager, you'll play a pivotal role in bringing this mission to life.

You'll lead the end-to-end delivery of key products and propositions at a portfolio level, ensuring work flows efficiently, predictably, and in line with our strategic goals. A key part of your role is optimising delivery performance-prioritising initiatives for maximum value, removing blockers, and ensuring all work is accurately assessed, governed, and reported.

Working across multiple squads, you'll partner closely with Product Leads, Heads of Value Streams, Technology, and cross-functional teams to drive delivery excellence and embed continuous improvement.

You will also shape and uplift the organisation's delivery capability, leading and developing Business Analysts, Delivery Leads, and other practitioners to build a culture grounded in accountability, collaboration, and growth.

What you'll be doing:

Drive the delivery rhythm, prioritisation, and sequencing of work to ensure products and propositions land predictably and deliver strategic value. Act as a key escalation point for trade-off decisions between initiatives, ensuring issues are resolved quickly and effectively.
Embed AGL's system of work, agile practices, and governance standards to drive high-quality outcomes. Use delivery insights and performance data to identify systemic blockers, uplift predictability, and lead continuous improvement initiatives across the value streams.
Lead, coach, and develop Business Analysts, Delivery Leads, and delivery practitioners. Foster a culture of accountability, innovation, and psychological safety while evolving frameworks, standards, and best practices that scale across teams.
Ensure clear visibility of delivery progress, dependencies, and risks at a portfolio level. Monitor key metrics, provide data-driven insights, and present recommendations that support effective decision-making and maximise value.
Partner closely with Product Leads, Value Stream leaders, Technology, and external delivery partners to ensure strategic alignment, manage dependencies, and maintain consistent delivery quality across the portfolio.

About you:

Previous demonstrated experience leading delivery across multiple initiatives across value streams, accountable for end-to-end outcomes.
Demonstrated ability to lead, coach and develop delivery professionals.
Deep understanding of Agile delivery frameworks (Scrum, Kanban, HCD).
Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills, with proven success aligning multiple teams toward shared outcomes.
Experience managing risks, dependencies and impediments across complex or cross-functional programs of work.

At AGL, we offer a wide range of benefits including:

Flexible working options including hybrid work.
Discounts on energy, telcos and solar plans.
Participate in Energise - our reward & recognition program.
Access 20 weeks of paid parental leave for the primary carer.
Access to an additional week of recharge leave per year.
Play an active role in our diversity & inclusion initiatives.
Novated leasing and Electric Vehicle subscriptions.
Access to study assistance, AGL's share purchase plan and insurance deals.

This role sits within one of Australia’s leading cultural institutions, where major investment is underway to modernise the systems that manage and share its collections.

The Opportunity

This is a rare and unique opportunity to help stabilise and modernise the data infrastructure behind one of Australia’s most diverse and fascinating datasets.

The team has recently taken ownership of a complex ecosystem of legacy collection systems and bespoke integrations.

Funding has now been secured to stabilise the environment, document the architecture and prepare the foundations for a modern data platform.

You’ll play a critical role untangling existing data pipelines and APIs while helping shape the future integration architecture.

This role provides the opportunity to:

Solve complex data and integration challenges across legacy platforms and bespoke APIs
Work with some of the most interesting and unique datasets you’ll come across
Contribute to an organisation that genuinely makes a positive impact on Australian society
Be part of some of the most unique and enjoyable team discovery sessions you can imagine
Work across multiple Canberra locations (with free parking at one site)
The Role

This role plays a key part in stabilising and improving the data pipelines and integration platforms as the first stage of moving toward a modern data solution that you will also shape.

You’ll have the autonomy to investigate the current architecture, identify risks, and implement stabilisation improvements in a careful and considered way.

Key responsibilities include:

Mapping and documenting existing data architecture and ETL pipelines
Reviewing existing documentation and code where configurations and functions are outdated or causing bugs due to poor version control in Git
Identifying and resolving issues across legacy data flows and APIs
Building and maintaining data pipelines supporting collection systems
Improving data quality frameworks and integration standards
Enabling data extraction for reporting, analytics and public-facing access
Contributing to the longer-term modernisation of the integration platform
The analogy I would use is stabilising the architecture, plumbing and electrical of a house you’re living in carefully repairing the foundations, so it doesn’t collapse before preparing the house for a potential move to a new block.
 
The Ideal Person

This role will suit an experienced data specialist with skills across data engineering and data architecture who enjoys the challenge of working with legacy and bespoke systems rather than only cutting-edge technology.

You are methodical in your documentation (including code) and understand that when working on systems that resemble untangling Christmas lights that are still plugged in, you need to take a careful, risk-mitigating approach.

Due to the nature of the project, this role will suit someone with a stable career history and experience delivering data projects from start through to completion.

Australian citizenship with a Baseline clearance is mandatory.
 
Salary / Rate

This is a full-time contract likely to be funded through to 30/6/2027.

Rate guide depending on skills and experience: $130/hr - $150/hr.
 
How to Apply

To apply online, please click on the appropriate link.
 
Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Lucas Walker on 02 0483 911 561 quoting ref no. 21430

Ad Text:

This organisation is a well-known player in the Australian energy sector, currently delivering a number of technology and digital initiatives across their platforms. They are investing heavily in modernising internal systems and building new digital capabilities that support operational and customer-facing products.

Due to increased project demand, they are looking to engage an experienced Technical Business Analyst on a 6-month daily rate contract to work closely with engineering and product teams on a number of platform initiatives.

You will operate as a bridge between engineering teams, UX/design, and business stakeholders, ensuring requirements are clearly defined and translated into scalable technical solutions.

This is a hands-on Technical BA role suited to someone comfortable working closely with developers and understanding modern web architectures and data-driven systems.

Key Responsibilities

Work with business stakeholders to gather and document functional and technical requirements.

Translate requirements into clear user stories, technical specifications, and acceptance criteria.

Partner closely with software engineers, UX designers, and product teams to deliver high-quality solutions.

Support the design and delivery of modern applications using Python-based services and React front-end platforms.

Facilitate workshops, backlog refinement, and sprint ceremonies within Agile delivery teams.

Analyse existing systems and processes to identify opportunities for improvement and optimisation.

Ensure alignment between technical delivery and broader business objectives.

Key Skills Required:

Proven experience as a Technical Business Analyst within the energy sector (utilities, electricity markets, renewables, or energy trading environments).

Strong technical understanding with exposure to Python and React-based applications.

Experience working closely with software development teams in Agile environments.

Ability to confidently engage with developers, UX designers, and business stakeholders.

Strong documentation, communication, and stakeholder management skills.

Experience working on digital platforms, data products, or customer-facing applications.

Exposure to API-driven architectures or cloud environments.

Understanding of energy market systems or operational platforms.
